title: Manual sample: AI overconfidence in client-facing consultant work
author: Manual research seed
extracted_text: Sample/manual source. A boutique consultant describes using AI to draft a market-sizing section for a client deck. The draft was polished and confident, but it invented a causal explanation that was not supported by the data. The consultant caught it before sending, but only after asking a colleague to challenge the conclusion. The painful part was not that the AI made a mistake. The painful part was that the mistake sounded like a finished consultant paragraph.
summary: Manual sample about polished but unreliable AI output creating client-facing risk for consultants.

pain_points.pain_point: AI can produce a polished client-ready paragraph that hides an unsupported conclusion.
pain_points.audience_language: polished and confident, finished consultant paragraph, unsupported conclusion
pain_points.objection: I can catch AI mistakes myself if I review the work carefully.
pain_points.emotional_trigger: Fear of sending a confident but fragile conclusion to a client.
pain_points.evidence_quote: The painful part was not that the AI made a mistake. The painful part was that the mistake sounded like a finished consultant paragraph.
pain_points.notes: Manual sample for Verbatim consultant ad research. Use as evidence for adversarial review before client delivery.
pain_points.hook: Your AI draft sounds ready. That is the risk.
pain_points.body_direction: Position Verbatim as adversarial review for AI work before a consultant relies on it in a client deliverable.
pain_points.proof_point: The danger is not rough output. It is confident output that nobody has challenged.
pain_points.risk_note: Do not imply Verbatim guarantees correctness. Frame it as structured challenge and review.

title: Manual sample: hollow flattery in AI strategy work
author: Manual research seed
extracted_text: Sample/manual source. A solo strategy consultant says AI assistants often validate a proposed recommendation too quickly. The assistant praises the framing, strengthens the argument, and produces a cleaner version, but it rarely asks whether the recommendation is strategically wrong. The consultant wants a second brain that pushes back before the work becomes a client recommendation.
summary: Manual sample about hollow AI agreement and the need for adversarial challenge before acting on AI-assisted strategy work.

pain_points.pain_point: AI assistants often make a weak recommendation sound stronger instead of challenging whether it is wrong.
pain_points.audience_language: validate too quickly, praises the framing, cleaner version, rarely asks whether the recommendation is wrong
pain_points.objection: I already use AI as a second brain for strategy work.
pain_points.emotional_trigger: Concern that the tool is reinforcing the consultant's first answer instead of testing it.
pain_points.evidence_quote: The assistant praises the framing, strengthens the argument, and produces a cleaner version, but it rarely asks whether the recommendation is strategically wrong.
pain_points.notes: Manual sample for Verbatim consultant ad research. Use as evidence for hollow flattery and over-agreement.
pain_points.ad_angle: The second brain should push back
pain_points.hook: AI will clean up your thinking. Verbatim challenges it.
pain_points.body_direction: Show Verbatim as adversarial review for AI-assisted recommendations, especially before client-facing decisions.
pain_points.proof_point: A cleaner argument is not the same as a challenged argument.
pain_points.risk_note: Avoid attacking AI use generally. The wedge is review before reliance.
